title: Fix selinux test for exec
body:
We want to make sure that the process label of pid 1 is the same as the process label of a process execed into the container.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

That looks better, but I'm still confused: I can't find a way to trigger a test failure, even with 7318b0e8 (the predecessor commit). On all podmans I've tried, I never get a mismatch between `/1/` and `/self`:
```
# podman run --name foo -d alpine_labels sh -c "cat /proc/self/attr/current;echo;sleep 60"
8df4641d40c7652f72773085925358b62cc330661bbaaa22a4540a6666977bb3

! emulate the ginkgo test
# podman exec foo cat /proc/1/attr/current;echo
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c122,c852
# podman exec foo cat /proc/self/attr/current;echo
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c122,c852

! should be the same as /proc/1
# podman logs foo
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c122,c852
```
Under what circumstances would `exec` show different results?
It was broken in Fedora 31 on crun.  I saw it on my machine and we had a issue on it.#4361
Also try it with rootless containers perhaps.

Oh... crun. Thanks, I should've thought of that (I just don't have a build environment for f31). I did try rootless.

Anyhow, I just wanted to understand the context. This looks like a sensible test. Thanks for the explanation. Will LGTM once tests finish.
